name: Route of the Lakes
name_original: Ida y vuelta por el Embalse de Terradets
description: Easy 4.3-mile hike around Terradets Reservoir in Serra del Montsec, offering views of the gorge and Montsec mountains.
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails around Pantà de Terradets are set within a landscape defined by the dramatic Terradets Gorge, where the Noguera Pallaresa river cuts through the Montsec mountains. This region features spectacular limestone cliffs, including the prominent Roca Regina, and the central Pantà de Terradets reservoir. Along the reservoir's shores, hikers encounter reed swamps and bank forests, while further inland, the terrain transitions into shaded woods of pines and oaks. Best family friendly hiking trails around Pantà de Terradets The most popular family friendly hiking route is Barranc del Bosc and Roca Regina Trail, a 5.4 miles (8.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours to complete. This moderate route explores a gorge and provides views of Roca Regina and the Terradets Reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Lakes, an easy 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This trail features gentle gradients and offers scenic views of the local water bodies. Local hikers also love the View of Pantà de Terradets – Terradets Reservoir loop from Pantà de Terradets, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) trail leading through varied scenery around the reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Hiking around Pantà de Terradets is defined by limestone cliffs, the reservoir, and shaded woods, offering a diverse natural space. The network of routes provides options for different ability levels, including easy family hikes and more moderate trails. The routes in Pantà de Terradets are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Pantà de Terradets's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available around Pantà de Terradets?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;13 family-friendly hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt; around Pantà de Terradets featured in this guide. These routes offer a range of experiences, from easy strolls along the reservoir to more moderate paths exploring the dramatic gorge.

Q: Are there any easy, short walks suitable for families with young children or prams?
A: Yes, for a gentle experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084850146/route-of-the-lakes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Lakes&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 6.9 km path with minimal elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31855609" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Terradets Reservoir loop from Cellers&lt;/a&gt; is also a shorter, easy option at 5.1 km. Additionally, the region offers an adapted route of 3.3 km between "La Pineda" and the Monares bridge, passing through Cellers, which is accessible to visitors with reduced mobility and likely suitable for prams.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks we can see on these family hikes?
A: Many trails offer stunning views of the &lt;strong&gt;Pantà de Terradets reservoir&lt;/strong&gt; itself. You'll also encounter the impressive &lt;strong&gt;Terradets Gorge&lt;/strong&gt;, with its spectacular limestone cliffs, including the prominent &lt;strong&gt;Roca Regina&lt;/strong&gt;. The landscape features diverse environments, from reed swamps and bank forests along the water to shaded woods of pines and oaks further inland.

Q: Are there any circular routes suitable for families?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, providing a convenient hiking experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31858090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pantà de Terradets – Terradets Reservoir loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 7.1 km option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31855609" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Terradets Reservoir loop from Cellers&lt;/a&gt; offers a shorter 5.1 km circuit.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking with family in Pantà de Terradets?
A: The Pantà de Terradets area is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with spring bringing lush greenery and autumn showcasing rich colors. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, especially for routes with less shade. Winter walks are also possible, though some higher or less maintained paths might require more preparation.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the family-friendly trails around Pantà de Terradets?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in the Pantà de Terradets area. However, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to follow local regulations. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: What do other hikers say about the family-friendly trails in Pantà de Terradets?
A: The komoot community rates the family-friendly trails around Pantà de Terradets highly, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.4 out of 5 stars&lt;/strong&gt;. Hikers often praise the varied scenery, the stunning views of the reservoir and gorge, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable family outing.

Q: Are there any moderate trails for families looking for a bit more challenge?
A: For families seeking a slightly more challenging adventure, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930361878/barranc-del-bosc-and-roca-regina-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc del Bosc and Roca Regina Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.8 km route that explores a gorge and provides stunning views of the Roca Regina rock formations. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bird Observatory – Ermita de Sant Sebastià loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.9 km long.

Q: Where can we find parking for these family hikes?
A: Parking is generally available at common starting points for trails around Pantà de Terradets, such as near the reservoir itself or in villages like Cellers. Specific parking information can often be found in the details of individual komoot tours, so it's advisable to check the route page before you go.

Q: What should we pack for a family hike in Pantà de Terradets?
A: For a family hike, it's essential to pack plenty of water, snacks, and a first-aid kit. Depending on the season, layers of clothing are recommended, along with sun protection (hats, sunscreen) and sturdy, comfortable footwear. Don't forget a camera to capture the beautiful scenery, and perhaps binoculars for wildlife spotting.

Q: Are there good spots for a picnic along the family trails?
A: Yes, the scenic shores of the Pantà de Terradets reservoir and various shaded areas within the pine and oak woods offer many lovely spots for a family picnic. Look for designated picnic areas or simply find a comfortable, scenic spot to enjoy your meal amidst nature.
tips: Area of this area of the reservoir where it is common to see birds as it represents a wet area, full of biodiversity.
Beautiful reservoir with a great view of the mountains.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Round trip to the Terradets Reservoir?
faqs.answer: This easy hike typically takes around 1 hour and 45 minutes to complete. It's a relatively short and gentle route, perfect for a relaxed outing.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as easy, with minimal elevation gain and a distance of just under 7 kilometers. It's well-suited for beginners, families, and those looking for a leisurely stroll around the reservoir.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain around the Terradets Reservoir is varied. You'll experience paths alongside the reservoir, potentially passing through areas with reed swamps and bank forests. The wider region is known for its dramatic gorge and impressive cliffs, offering a beautiful backdrop to your walk.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: As you hike, you'll enjoy stunning views of the Terradets Reservoir itself. Keep an eye out for the Bird Observatory, and be sure to take in the panoramic View of Pantà de Terradets.

faqs.question: Is the Round trip to the Terradets Reservoir dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in this region. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail or the Serra del Montsec area?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a specific permit to hike this trail, nor are there any entrance fees for accessing the general hiking areas within the Serra del Montsec. Enjoy the natural beauty freely!

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for this exact starting point aren't provided, the Terradets Reservoir area generally offers parking options near popular access points. Look for designated parking areas close to the reservoir's edge or near any service stations in the vicinity.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Terradets Reservoir area is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be hot, so an early start is recommended if hiking during that season.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with sections of other trails in the area. You'll find it overlaps with parts of the Ruta dels Llacs, and also briefly with the Barranc del Bosc i Roca Regina trail.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The Terradets Reservoir area is a popular spot, and you may find amenities such as cafes or service stations nearby, particularly along the main roads that run close to the reservoir. These often provide restrooms and refreshments.

faqs.question: Is there public transport available to reach the Terradets Reservoir?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area is accessible by public transport. The "Tren dels Llacs" (Lakes Train) passes through this region and has a stop at Cellers station, which is a convenient way to reach the vicinity of the reservoir.
